facts.statement: Lowe's is a Fortune 100 company and the world's second largest home improvement retailer, operating 1,759 home improvement stores and outlets in the United States with approximately 196 million square feet of retail selling space as of January 30, 2026.

Lowe's operated over 540 branch locations in the United States and Canada as of January 30, 2026, including branch locations added through the 2025 acquisitions of Foundation Building Materials (FBM) and Artisan Design Group (ADG).

Lowe's Total Home strategy, updated in December 2024, has five pillars: drive Pro penetration, accelerate online sales, expand home services, create a loyalty ecosystem, and increase space productivity.

Lowe's serves individual homeowners and renters across the DIY and do-it-for-me (DIFM) spectrum, and Pro customers primarily consisting of small to medium-sized tradespeople, repair and remodelers, and property managers; with the FBM and ADG acquisitions, Lowe's is also serving larger Pro customers in residential and commercial markets.

In June 2025, Lowe's acquired Artisan Design Group (ADG), a nationwide provider of design, distribution and installation services for interior surface finishes including flooring, cabinets and countertops, with a home builder and property manager customer base.

In October 2025, Lowe's acquired Foundation Building Materials (FBM), a North American distributor of interior building products including drywall, metal framing, ceiling systems, commercial doors and hardware, insulation and complementary products serving large residential and commercial professionals.

FBM's aggregate assets (excluding goodwill and intangible assets net) and net sales represented 4.5% and 1.5% of Lowe's consolidated total assets and consolidated net sales, respectively, as of and for the year ended January 30, 2026.
Lowe's reported revenue of $86.377 billion for FY2025 (fiscal year ended January 30, 2026).
Lowe's reported net income of $7.726 billion for FY2025.
Lowe's generated operating cash flow of $8.14 billion for FY2025.
As of January 30, 2026, Lowe's had stockholders' equity of negative $14.254 billion, long-term debt of $39.819 billion, and cash and equivalents of $1.761 billion.

For FY2025, the Retail Home Improvement segment generated net sales of $84.078 billion with operating income of $10.223 billion (12.16% operating margin), while the Other segment generated net sales of $2.208 billion with an operating loss of $70 million.

Lowe's reported a consolidated operating margin of 11.77% for FY2025; adjusted operating margin was 12.11% after excluding $293 million in pre-tax transaction costs and intangible asset amortization related to the ADG and FBM acquisitions.

Lowe's returned $2.6 billion in dividends to shareholders in FY2025 and has paid $12.1 billion in dividends over the last five years; the company has also repurchased $37.4 billion in shares over the last five years.
Lowe's generated $46.3 billion in cash flows from operations over the last five years and achieved a 2025 return on invested capital (ROIC) of 26.1%.
Lowe's is a Dividend Aristocrat and increased its annual dividend per share by 4.4% in 2025.

Marvin R. Ellison serves as Chairman, President and Chief Executive Officer of Lowe's; Brandon J. Sink serves as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer; and Dan C. Griggs, Jr. serves as Senior Vice President, Tax and Chief Accounting Officer.

Lowe's Board of Directors consists of 12 nominees including Marvin R. Ellison as Chairman, President and CEO, and Richard W. Dreiling as Lead Independent Director; other directors include Raul Alvarez, Scott H. Baxter, Sandra B. Cochran, Laurie Z. Douglas, Navdeep Gupta, Brian C. Rogers, Bertram L. Scott, Lawrence Simkins, Colleen Taylor, and Mary Beth West.
Approximately 13% of Lowe's retail sales are online, and the company has approximately 300,000 associates.

Lowe's reports operating segment merchandise categories including Appliances, Seasonal and Outdoor Living, Lumber, Lawn and Garden, Hardware, Kitchens and Bath, Building Materials, Rough Plumbing, Paint, Millwork, Tools, Electrical, Flooring, and Decor.

On February 20, 2026, the Supreme Court declared that tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) were invalid, and on March 4, 2026, the Court of International Trade ordered U.S. Customs and Border Protection to liquidate entries without regard to IEEPA duties; Lowe's is evaluating the impact on its consolidated financial position, results of operations, and cash flows.

Lowe's is undergoing a multi-year technology transformation which includes updating and modernizing its distribution and replenishment systems, as well as certain accounting and finance systems, with updates expected to continue for the next few years.
As of March 19, 2026, Lowe's had 560,063,429 shares of common stock outstanding with a par value of $0.50 per share.

Lowe's competes with national and regional home improvement warehouse chains and lumber yards, as well as traditional hardware, plumbing, electrical, drywall and home specialty stores in most of the markets it serves.
